text.title: ASP.NET Core: BackgroundService Patterns
text.summary: BackgroundService + Channel<T> for in-process queued work, and PeriodicTimer for scheduled cleanup tasks in ASP.NET Core.

rule.statement: Use BackgroundService + Channel<T> for in-process background work. Inject the Channel as a Singleton; create a scoped IServiceProvider scope inside ExecuteAsync for each work item to avoid capturing a scoped DbContext in a singleton lifetime.
rule.rationale: DbContext is Scoped by default. Injecting it directly into a Singleton (the BackgroundService) captures a Scoped dependency in a longer-lived object — EF Core will throw at runtime on the second request.

example.note: Register Channel<int> as Singleton and IOrderQueue (the writer facade) as Singleton in Program.cs. The BackgroundService is auto-discovered by AddHostedService<T>.

rule.statement: Use PeriodicTimer inside BackgroundService for periodic scheduled tasks. Never use Thread.Sleep or Task.Delay in a loop — PeriodicTimer handles scheduling and respects CancellationToken.
rule.rationale: PeriodicTimer.WaitForNextTickAsync() fires at the correct wall-clock interval even if a tick was slow, does not accumulate drift, and returns false when the token is cancelled — enabling clean shutdown without extra coordination.
